• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code

LOCUS-MENTAL Eye Tracking Battery Validation

Iskra Todorova & Nico Bast

2026-04-22

1 Setup

1.1 Package Installation & Loading

pkgs <- c(
  "tidyverse", "GGally", "lme4", "emmeans", "lmerTest",
  "dplyr", "corrplot", "factoextra", "ggpubr", "Hmisc", "kableExtra", "broom"
)

installed_packages <- pkgs %in% rownames(installed.packages())
if (any(!installed_packages)) {
  install.packages(pkgs[!installed_packages])
}

lapply(pkgs, function(pkg) {
  if (!require(pkg, character.only = TRUE, quietly = TRUE)) {
    message(paste("Package", pkg, "could not be loaded."))
  }
})
## [[1]]
## NULL
## 
## [[2]]
## NULL

1.2 File Paths

home_path      <- "S:/KJP_Studien"
data_path_vo   <- "/LOCUS_MENTAL/6_Versuchsdaten/visual_oddball/"
data_path_ao   <- "/LOCUS_MENTAL/6_Versuchsdaten/auditory_oddball/"
data_path_rss  <- "/LOCUS_MENTAL/6_Versuchsdaten/rapid_sound_sequences/"
data_path_cvs  <- "/LOCUS_MENTAL/6_Versuchsdaten/visual_search_task/"
demo_data_path <- "S:/KJP_Studien/LOCUS_MENTAL/6_Versuchsdaten/"

2 Data Loading

# Aggregated task data
df_ao  <- readRDS(paste0(home_path, data_path_ao,  "df_sepr_aggregated_AO.rds"))
df_vo  <- readRDS(paste0(home_path, data_path_vo,  "sepr_condition.rds"))
df_rss <- readRDS(paste0(home_path, data_path_rss, "df_sepr_aggregated_RSS.rds"))
df_cvs <- readRDS(paste0(home_path, data_path_cvs, "df_cued_agg.rds"))

# Baseline pupil size (BPS) trial-level data
bps_rss <- readRDS(paste0(home_path, data_path_rss, "bps_data.rds"))
bps_ao  <- readRDS(paste0(home_path, data_path_ao,  "bps_data.rds"))
bps_cvs <- readRDS(paste0(home_path, data_path_cvs, "bps_data.rds"))

# Demographic data
load(file.path(demo_data_path, "demo_data.rda"))
demo_data <- data

3 Data Preparation

Each task dataset is cleaned and standardised before being joined into a single master dataframe.

3.1 Auditory Oddball (AO)

data_ao <- df_ao %>%
  select(id, SEPR_AO_m_oddball, SEPR_AO_m_standard, BPS_oddball, BPS_standard) %>%
  rename(
    BPS_AO_standard = BPS_standard,
    BPS_AO_oddball  = BPS_oddball
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    SEPR_AO_standard_z = as.numeric(scale(SEPR_AO_m_standard)),
    SEPR_AO_oddball_z  = as.numeric(scale(SEPR_AO_m_oddball))
  )

3.2 Rapid Sound Sequences (RSS)

data_rss <- df_rss %>%
  select(
    id,
    RSS_SEPR_early_mean_control, RSS_SEPR_early_mean_transition,
    RSS_BPS_mean_control, RSS_BPS_mean_transition,
    mean_diff_RAND_to_REG1, mean_diff_REG_to_RAND, mean_diff_RAND_to_REG10
  ) %>%
  rename(
    BPR_RSS_control    = RSS_BPS_mean_control,
    BPS_RSS_transition = RSS_BPS_mean_transition
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    RSS_transition_z = as.numeric(scale(RSS_SEPR_early_mean_transition)),
    RSS_control_z    = as.numeric(scale(RSS_SEPR_early_mean_control))
  )

3.3 Visual Oddball (VO)

# Pivot from long to wide; retain only participants with >= 7 trials in both conditions
data_vo <- df_vo %>%
  filter(n_trials >= 7) %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  filter(n() == 2) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(sepr_scaled = as.numeric(scale(sepr_mean))) %>%
  select(id, condition, sepr_mean, sepr_scaled, BPS, n_trials) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = condition,
    values_from = c(sepr_mean, sepr_scaled, BPS, n_trials),
    names_glue  = "{.value}_VO_{condition}"
  )

cat("Participants remaining in VO task:", nrow(data_vo), "\n")
## Participants remaining in VO task: 74

3.4 Cued Visual Search (CVS)

data_cvs <- df_cvs %>%
  select(
    id,
    CEPR_CUED_mean_cued, CEPR_CUED_mean_standard,
    SEPR_mean_cued, SEPR_mean_standard,
    BPS_cued, BPS_standard
  )

3.5 Master Dataframe

# Full join retains all participants; missing tasks appear as NAs.
# VO excluded pending data structure harmonisation — see note above.
df_final <- data_ao %>%
  full_join(data_rss, by = "id") %>%
  # full_join(data_vo, by = "id") %>%
  full_join(data_cvs, by = "id")

cat("Total participants in master dataframe:", nrow(df_final), "\n")
## Total participants in master dataframe: 99

Note: The VO task is currently excluded from the master dataframe because the task effects did not worked out as expected probably due to task structure. However the BPS is included in the analysis. Re-enable the commented full_join line and remove the VO row from the BPS summary if the data is needed —

4 Baseline Pupil Size (BPS)

BPS reflects individual differences in tonic arousal and is expected to be highly stable within a person across tasks.

4.1 Within-Battery Consistency

bps_matrix <- df_final %>% select(id, starts_with("BPS"))

cor_bps <- cor(
  bps_matrix %>% select(-id),
  use    = "pairwise.complete.obs",
  method = "spearman"
)

cat("Spearman Correlations of Baseline Pupil Size Across Tasks:\n")
## Spearman Correlations of Baseline Pupil Size Across Tasks:
print(round(cor_bps, 2))
##                    BPS_AO_oddball BPS_AO_standard BPS_RSS_transition BPS_cued
## BPS_AO_oddball               1.00            0.99               0.88     0.86
## BPS_AO_standard              0.99            1.00               0.90     0.88
## BPS_RSS_transition           0.88            0.90               1.00     0.92
## BPS_cued                     0.86            0.88               0.92     1.00
## BPS_standard                 0.87            0.88               0.92     0.97
##                    BPS_standard
## BPS_AO_oddball             0.87
## BPS_AO_standard            0.88
## BPS_RSS_transition         0.92
## BPS_cued                   0.97
## BPS_standard               1.00
corrplot(
  cor_bps,
  method      = "color",
  type        = "upper",
  tl.col      = "black",
  tl.srt      = 45,
  addCoef.col = "black",
  number.cex  = 0.8,
  col         = colorRampPalette(c("#2166AC", "white", "#B2182B"))(200),
  title       = "BPS Cross-Task Consistency (Spearman)",
  mar         = c(0, 0, 2, 0)
)

4.2 BPS Descriptive Summary

bps_summary <- data.frame(
  Task     = c("AO", "RSS", "CVS"),
  Mean_BPS = c(
    mean(df_final$BPS_AO_standard,    na.rm = TRUE),
    mean(df_final$BPS_RSS_transition, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean(df_final$BPS_cued,           na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  SD_BPS = c(
    sd(df_final$BPS_AO_standard,    na.rm = TRUE),
    sd(df_final$BPS_RSS_transition, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd(df_final$BPS_cued,           na.rm = TRUE)
  )
)

bps_summary %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))) %>%
  kable(caption = "Mean Baseline Pupil Size (mm) per Task") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE)
Mean Baseline Pupil Size (mm) per Task
Task Mean_BPS SD_BPS
AO 3.642 0.471
RSS 3.629 0.429
CVS 3.491 0.410

5 Habituation Analysis

Mixed-effects models test whether BPS drifts systematically over the course of each task. Expectation: negative estimates

5.1 BPS Trajectory Plots

Trial-by-trial mean BPS for each task. A downward trend indicates habituation; a flat profile indicates stable arousal across the session.

# AO
ao_traj <- bps_ao %>%
  group_by(trial_number) %>%
  summarise(mean_BPS = mean(BPS, na.rm = TRUE),
            se_BPS   = sd(BPS, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
            .groups  = "drop") %>%
  mutate(Task = "Auditory Oddball")

# CVS
cvs_traj <- bps_cvs %>%
  group_by(trial_number) %>%
  summarise(mean_BPS = mean(BPS, na.rm = TRUE),
            se_BPS   = sd(BPS, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
            .groups  = "drop") %>%
  mutate(Task = "Cued Visual Search")

# RSS
rss_traj <- bps_rss %>%
  rename(trial_number = Trial.Number) %>%
  group_by(trial_number) %>%
  summarise(mean_BPS = mean(BPS, na.rm = TRUE),
            se_BPS   = sd(BPS, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
            .groups  = "drop") %>%
  mutate(Task = "Rapid Sound Sequences")

bind_rows(ao_traj, cvs_traj, rss_traj) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = trial_number, y = mean_BPS)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mean_BPS - se_BPS, ymax = mean_BPS + se_BPS),
              fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_line(colour = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", colour = "red", linetype = "dashed",
              linewidth = 0.7, se = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ Task, scales = "free_x") +
  labs(
    title   = "Baseline Pupil Size Over Trials (Mean ± SE)",
    x       = "Trial Number",
    y       = "Mean BPS (mm)",
    caption = "Red dashed line = linear trend fit. Shaded band = ±1 SE across participants."
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90"))

5.2 Auditory Oddball

bps_ao$condition <- as.factor(bps_ao$condition)

m_ao_hab <- lmer(BPS ~ trial_number + (1 | id), data = bps_ao)
cat("--- AO: Main effect of trial number ---\n")
## --- AO: Main effect of trial number ---
print(anova(m_ao_hab))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##              Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## trial_number 47.073  47.073     1 9215.4  600.02 < 2.2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
m_ao_int <- lmer(BPS ~ trial_number * condition + (1 | id), data = bps_ao)
cat("--- AO: Trial number x Condition interaction ---\n")
## --- AO: Trial number x Condition interaction ---
print(anova(m_ao_int))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                         Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF  F value Pr(>F)    
## trial_number           31.2103 31.2103     1 9213.3 397.7901 <2e-16 ***
## condition               0.0732  0.0732     1 9213.0   0.9331 0.3341    
## trial_number:condition  0.0322  0.0322     1 9213.0   0.4110 0.5215    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

5.3 Cued Visual Search

bps_cvs$Condition    <- as.factor(bps_cvs$Condition)
bps_cvs$trial_number <- as.numeric(bps_cvs$trial_number)

m_cvs_hab <- lmer(BPS ~ trial_number + (1 | id), data = bps_cvs)
cat("--- CVS: Main effect of trial number ---\n")
## --- CVS: Main effect of trial number ---
print(anova(m_cvs_hab))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                 Sum Sq   Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value Pr(>F)
## trial_number 0.0088373 0.0088373     1 2768.5  0.1478 0.7007
m_cvs_int <- lmer(BPS ~ trial_number * Condition + (1 | id), data = bps_cvs)
cat("--- CVS: Trial number x Condition interaction ---\n")
## --- CVS: Trial number x Condition interaction ---
print(anova(m_cvs_int))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq   Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value Pr(>F)
## trial_number           0.0098614 0.0098614     1 2766.5  0.1648 0.6848
## Condition              0.0184609 0.0184609     1 2767.9  0.3085 0.5786
## trial_number:Condition 0.0215491 0.0215491     1 2767.6  0.3601 0.5485

5.4 Rapid Sound Sequences

bps_rss$Condition    <- as.factor(bps_rss$Condition)
bps_rss$Trial.Number <- as.numeric(bps_rss$Trial.Number)

m_rss_hab <- lmer(BPS ~ Trial.Number + (1 | id), data = bps_rss)
cat("--- RSS: Main effect of trial number ---\n")
## --- RSS: Main effect of trial number ---
print(anova(m_rss_hab))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##               Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value Pr(>F)
## Trial.Number 0.04496 0.04496     1 2566.4  0.7323 0.3922
m_rss_int <- lmer(BPS ~ Trial.Number * Condition + (1 | id), data = bps_rss)
cat("--- RSS: Trial number x Condition interaction ---\n")
## --- RSS: Trial number x Condition interaction ---
print(anova(m_rss_int))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                          Sum Sq  Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value Pr(>F)
## Trial.Number           0.014101 0.014101     1 2558.5  0.2293 0.6321
## Condition              0.147199 0.036800     4 2558.0  0.5985 0.6638
## Trial.Number:Condition 0.085415 0.021354     4 2558.3  0.3473 0.8461

6 Correlation Analysis

Method note: Spearman rank correlations are used throughout as the primary measure, as pupil dilation data may deviate from normality.

6.1 Build Correlation Dataset

df_cor_rpd_raw <- df_final %>%
  select(
    id,
    SEPR_AO_m_oddball, SEPR_AO_m_standard,                                  # AO
    RSS_SEPR_early_mean_control, RSS_SEPR_early_mean_transition,             # RSS SEPR
    mean_diff_RAND_to_REG1, mean_diff_REG_to_RAND, mean_diff_RAND_to_REG10, # RSS diffs
    SEPR_mean_cued, SEPR_mean_standard,                                      # CVS SEPR
    CEPR_CUED_mean_cued, CEPR_CUED_mean_standard                             # CVS CEPR
  )

cat("N participants in correlation dataset:", nrow(df_cor_rpd_raw), "\n")
## N participants in correlation dataset: 99

6.2 Spearman Correlation Matrix with FDR-Corrected p-values

Hmisc::rcorr() computes r and p simultaneously. p-values are then corrected for multiple comparisons using the Benjamini-Hochberg (FDR) procedure.

cor_obj <- Hmisc::rcorr(
  as.matrix(df_cor_rpd_raw %>% select(-id)),
  type = "spearman"
)

r_mat <- cor_obj$r
p_mat <- cor_obj$P

# Apply BH-FDR correction to all upper-triangle p-values
p_fdr_mat <- matrix(NA, nrow = nrow(p_mat), ncol = ncol(p_mat),
                    dimnames = dimnames(p_mat))
p_fdr_vec <- p.adjust(p_mat[upper.tri(p_mat)], method = "BH")
p_fdr_mat[upper.tri(p_fdr_mat)] <- p_fdr_vec
p_fdr_mat[lower.tri(p_fdr_mat)] <- t(p_fdr_mat)[lower.tri(p_fdr_mat)]
diag(p_fdr_mat)                 <- 1  # diagonal has no p-value; set to 1 so corrplot ignores it

cat("Spearman r matrix:\n")
## Spearman r matrix:
print(round(r_mat, 2))
##                                SEPR_AO_m_oddball SEPR_AO_m_standard
## SEPR_AO_m_oddball                           1.00              -0.06
## SEPR_AO_m_standard                         -0.06               1.00
## RSS_SEPR_early_mean_control                 0.00               0.15
## RSS_SEPR_early_mean_transition              0.05               0.06
## mean_diff_RAND_to_REG1                     -0.14               0.05
## mean_diff_REG_to_RAND                       0.12              -0.11
## mean_diff_RAND_to_REG10                    -0.19              -0.02
## SEPR_mean_cued                             -0.12               0.17
## SEPR_mean_standard                         -0.17               0.03
## CEPR_CUED_mean_cued                        -0.15               0.16
## CEPR_CUED_mean_standard                    -0.10              -0.01
##                                RSS_SEPR_early_mean_control
## SEPR_AO_m_oddball                                     0.00
## SEPR_AO_m_standard                                    0.15
## RSS_SEPR_early_mean_control                           1.00
## RSS_SEPR_early_mean_transition                        0.01
## mean_diff_RAND_to_REG1                               -0.41
## mean_diff_REG_to_RAND                                -0.37
## mean_diff_RAND_to_REG10                              -0.50
## SEPR_mean_cued                                       -0.04
## SEPR_mean_standard                                    0.05
## CEPR_CUED_mean_cued                                   0.10
## CEPR_CUED_mean_standard                               0.16
##                                RSS_SEPR_early_mean_transition
## SEPR_AO_m_oddball                                        0.05
## SEPR_AO_m_standard                                       0.06
## RSS_SEPR_early_mean_control                              0.01
## RSS_SEPR_early_mean_transition                           1.00
## mean_diff_RAND_to_REG1                                   0.44
## mean_diff_REG_to_RAND                                    0.32
## mean_diff_RAND_to_REG10                                  0.30
## SEPR_mean_cued                                           0.00
## SEPR_mean_standard                                       0.02
## CEPR_CUED_mean_cued                                      0.07
## CEPR_CUED_mean_standard                                 -0.08
##                                mean_diff_RAND_to_REG1 mean_diff_REG_to_RAND
## SEPR_AO_m_oddball                               -0.14                  0.12
## SEPR_AO_m_standard                               0.05                 -0.11
## RSS_SEPR_early_mean_control                     -0.41                 -0.37
## RSS_SEPR_early_mean_transition                   0.44                  0.32
## mean_diff_RAND_to_REG1                           1.00                 -0.15
## mean_diff_REG_to_RAND                           -0.15                  1.00
## mean_diff_RAND_to_REG10                          0.55                 -0.20
## SEPR_mean_cued                                   0.22                 -0.14
## SEPR_mean_standard                               0.07                 -0.05
## CEPR_CUED_mean_cued                              0.12                 -0.15
## CEPR_CUED_mean_standard                         -0.10                 -0.01
##                                mean_diff_RAND_to_REG10 SEPR_mean_cued
## SEPR_AO_m_oddball                                -0.19          -0.12
## SEPR_AO_m_standard                               -0.02           0.17
## RSS_SEPR_early_mean_control                      -0.50          -0.04
## RSS_SEPR_early_mean_transition                    0.30           0.00
## mean_diff_RAND_to_REG1                            0.55           0.22
## mean_diff_REG_to_RAND                            -0.20          -0.14
## mean_diff_RAND_to_REG10                           1.00           0.11
## SEPR_mean_cued                                    0.11           1.00
## SEPR_mean_standard                               -0.01           0.56
## CEPR_CUED_mean_cued                               0.11           0.39
## CEPR_CUED_mean_standard                          -0.14           0.16
##                                SEPR_mean_standard CEPR_CUED_mean_cued
## SEPR_AO_m_oddball                           -0.17               -0.15
## SEPR_AO_m_standard                           0.03                0.16
## RSS_SEPR_early_mean_control                  0.05                0.10
## RSS_SEPR_early_mean_transition               0.02                0.07
## mean_diff_RAND_to_REG1                       0.07                0.12
## mean_diff_REG_to_RAND                       -0.05               -0.15

##                                CEPR_CUED_mean_standard
## SEPR_AO_m_oddball                                -0.10

## CEPR_CUED_mean_cued                               0.22
## CEPR_CUED_mean_standard                           1.00
cat("\nFDR-corrected p-value matrix (BH):\n")
## 
## FDR-corrected p-value matrix (BH):
print(round(p_fdr_mat, 3))
##                                SEPR_AO_m_oddball SEPR_AO_m_standard
## SEPR_AO_m_oddball                          1.000              0.755
## SEPR_AO_m_standard                         0.755              1.000
## RSS_SEPR_early_mean_control                0.994              0.476
## RSS_SEPR_early_mean_transition             0.851              0.833
## mean_diff_RAND_to_REG1                     0.490              0.833
## mean_diff_REG_to_RAND                      0.562              0.591
## mean_diff_RAND_to_REG10                    0.356              0.968
## SEPR_mean_cued                             0.514              0.356
## SEPR_mean_standard                         0.356              0.945
## CEPR_CUED_mean_cued                        0.445              0.388
## CEPR_CUED_mean_standard                    0.591              0.968
##                                RSS_SEPR_early_mean_control
## SEPR_AO_m_oddball                                    0.994
## SEPR_AO_m_standard                                   0.476
## RSS_SEPR_early_mean_control                          1.000
## RSS_SEPR_early_mean_transition                       0.984
## mean_diff_RAND_to_REG1                               0.001
## mean_diff_REG_to_RAND                                0.006
## mean_diff_RAND_to_REG10                              0.000

## CEPR_CUED_mean_standard                              0.446

## SEPR_AO_m_standard                                      0.833
## RSS_SEPR_early_mean_control                             0.984

## mean_diff_RAND_to_REG10                                 0.039
## SEPR_mean_cued                                          0.994
## SEPR_mean_standard                                      0.968
## CEPR_CUED_mean_cued                                     0.755
## CEPR_CUED_mean_standard                                 0.744
##                                mean_diff_RAND_to_REG1 mean_diff_REG_to_RAND
## SEPR_AO_m_oddball                               0.490                 0.562
## SEPR_AO_m_standard                              0.833                 0.591
## RSS_SEPR_early_mean_control                     0.001                 0.006
## RSS_SEPR_early_mean_transition                  0.001                 0.029
## mean_diff_RAND_to_REG1                          1.000                 0.477
## mean_diff_REG_to_RAND                           0.477                 1.000
## mean_diff_RAND_to_REG10                         0.000                 0.355
## SEPR_mean_cued                                  0.243                 0.490
## SEPR_mean_standard                              0.753                 0.834
## CEPR_CUED_mean_cued                             0.562                 0.490
## CEPR_CUED_mean_standard                         0.591                 0.968
##                                mean_diff_RAND_to_REG10 SEPR_mean_cued
## SEPR_AO_m_oddball                                0.356          0.514
## SEPR_AO_m_standard                               0.968          0.356
## RSS_SEPR_early_mean_control                      0.000          0.856
## RSS_SEPR_early_mean_transition                   0.039          0.994
## mean_diff_RAND_to_REG1                           0.000          0.243
## mean_diff_REG_to_RAND                            0.355          0.490
## mean_diff_RAND_to_REG10                          1.000          0.591
## SEPR_mean_cued                                   0.591          1.000
## SEPR_mean_standard                               0.984          0.000
## CEPR_CUED_mean_cued                              0.591          0.001
## CEPR_CUED_mean_standard                          0.490          0.388
##                                SEPR_mean_standard CEPR_CUED_mean_cued
## SEPR_AO_m_oddball                           0.356               0.445
## SEPR_AO_m_standard                          0.945               0.388
## RSS_SEPR_early_mean_control                 0.833               0.591
## RSS_SEPR_early_mean_transition              0.968               0.755
## mean_diff_RAND_to_REG1                      0.753               0.562
## mean_diff_REG_to_RAND                       0.834               0.490
## mean_diff_RAND_to_REG10                     0.984               0.591
## SEPR_mean_cued                              0.000               0.001
## SEPR_mean_standard                          1.000               0.733
## CEPR_CUED_mean_cued                         0.733               1.000
## CEPR_CUED_mean_standard                     0.029               0.139
##                                CEPR_CUED_mean_standard
## SEPR_AO_m_oddball                                0.591
## SEPR_AO_m_standard                               0.968
## RSS_SEPR_early_mean_control                      0.446
## RSS_SEPR_early_mean_transition                   0.744
## mean_diff_RAND_to_REG1                           0.591
## mean_diff_REG_to_RAND                            0.968
## mean_diff_RAND_to_REG10                          0.490
## SEPR_mean_cued                                   0.388
## SEPR_mean_standard                               0.029
## CEPR_CUED_mean_cued                              0.139
## CEPR_CUED_mean_standard                          1.000
# 1. Get the number of variables to help with positioning
n <- ncol(r_mat)

# 2. Run corrplot WITHOUT addCoef.col
corrplot(
  r_mat,
  method      = "color",
  type        = "upper",
  tl.col      = "black",
  tl.srt      = 45,
  tl.cex      = 0.8,
  addCoef.col = NULL,      # Change this to NULL to stop the overlap
  p.mat       = p_fdr_mat,
  sig.level   = 0.05,
  insig       = "pch",   
  pch         = 8,       
  pch.cex     = 1.2,      # Slightly smaller stars help readability
  pch.col     = "black",
  col         = colorRampPalette(c("#2166AC", "white", "#B2182B"))(200),
  title       = "Spearman Correlations (stars = FDR p < .05)",
  mar         = c(0, 0, 2, 0)
)

# 3. Manually add the coefficients with a slight downward offset
# This loops through the upper triangle (where column j > row i)
for (i in 1:(n-1)) {
  for (j in (i+1):n) {
    # x is the column (j), y is the row (reversed for corrplot: n - i + 1)
    # We use 'pos = 1' to place the text directly below the center point
    text(x = j, 
         y = n - i + 1, 
         labels = round(r_mat[i, j], 2), 
         pos = 1,       # pos=1 puts text BELOW the center; pos=3 puts it ABOVE
         offset = 0.6,  # adjust this number to move the text further up or down
         cex = 0.6, 
         col = "black")
  }
}

6.3 Significant Correlation Pairs (FDR p < .05)

vars <- colnames(r_mat)

data.frame(
  Var1  = vars[row(r_mat)[upper.tri(r_mat)]],
  Var2  = vars[col(r_mat)[upper.tri(r_mat)]],
  r     = round(r_mat[upper.tri(r_mat)], 3),
  p_fdr = round(p_fdr_mat[upper.tri(p_fdr_mat)], 4)
) %>%
  filter(p_fdr < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(p_fdr) %>%
  kable(caption = "Significant Spearman Correlations (FDR-corrected p < .05)") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE)
Significant Spearman Correlations (FDR-corrected p < .05)
Var1 Var2 r p_fdr
RSS_SEPR_early_mean_control mean_diff_RAND_to_REG10 -0.504 0.0000
mean_diff_RAND_to_REG1 mean_diff_RAND_to_REG10 0.552 0.0000
SEPR_mean_cued SEPR_mean_standard 0.564 0.0000
RSS_SEPR_early_mean_transition mean_diff_RAND_to_REG1 0.440 0.0008
SEPR_mean_cued CEPR_CUED_mean_cued 0.392 0.0008
RSS_SEPR_early_mean_control mean_diff_RAND_to_REG1 -0.415 0.0015
RSS_SEPR_early_mean_control mean_diff_REG_to_RAND -0.372 0.0063
RSS_SEPR_early_mean_transition mean_diff_REG_to_RAND 0.318 0.0285
SEPR_mean_standard CEPR_CUED_mean_standard 0.285 0.0285
RSS_SEPR_early_mean_transition mean_diff_RAND_to_REG10 0.302 0.0391

6.4 Pairwise Scatter Plot Matrix (GGally)

ggpairs(
  df_cor_rpd_raw %>% select(-id),
  columns = 1:(ncol(df_cor_rpd_raw) - 1),
  upper   = list(continuous = wrap("cor", method = "spearman", size = 3)),
  lower   = list(continuous = wrap("points", alpha = 0.3, size = 0.8)),
  diag    = list(continuous = wrap("densityDiag"))
) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 9) +
  labs(title = "Battery Validation: Spearman Correlations Across Pupil Tasks")

6.5 Cross-Task Summary: Primary Condition Means

The most direct test of convergent validity: Spearman correlations between the single primary outcome from each task (AO Oddball, RSS Transition, CVS Cued).

cross_task_cor <- df_final %>%
  select(
    AO_Oddball     = SEPR_AO_m_oddball,
    RSS_Transition = RSS_SEPR_early_mean_transition,
    Cued_Task      = CEPR_CUED_mean_cued
  ) %>%
  as.matrix() %>%
  Hmisc::rcorr(type = "spearman")

cat("Cross-task r:\n")
## Cross-task r:
print(round(cross_task_cor$r, 2))
##                AO_Oddball RSS_Transition Cued_Task
## AO_Oddball           1.00           0.05     -0.15
## RSS_Transition       0.05           1.00      0.07
## Cued_Task           -0.15           0.07      1.00
cat("\nCross-task p (uncorrected — only 3 pairs):\n")
## 
## Cross-task p (uncorrected — only 3 pairs):
print(round(cross_task_cor$P, 3))
##                AO_Oddball RSS_Transition Cued_Task
## AO_Oddball             NA          0.696     0.154
## RSS_Transition      0.696             NA     0.547
## Cued_Task           0.154          0.547        NA

7 Principal Component Analysis (PCA)

PCA summarises the shared variance structure across all task conditions and helps identify whether tasks cluster into interpretable groups or measure largely independent processes.

7.1 Run PCA

pca_data       <- df_cor_rpd_raw[, 2:ncol(df_cor_rpd_raw)]
pca_data_clean <- na.omit(pca_data)

cat("N entering PCA (complete cases):", nrow(pca_data_clean),
    "of", nrow(pca_data), "total\n")
## N entering PCA (complete cases): 74 of 99 total
pca_result <- prcomp(pca_data_clean, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

7.2 Scree Plot

fviz_eig(pca_result, addlabels = TRUE, ncp = 6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 100 / ncol(pca_data_clean),
             linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  labs(
    title   = "Scree Plot: Variance Explained by Each Component",
    caption = "Dashed line = variance expected per variable under a null model (equal share)"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

7.3 Variable Factor Map

fviz_pca_var(
  pca_result,
  col.var       = "contrib",
  gradient.cols = c("#00AFBB", "#E7B800", "#FC4E07"),
  repel         = TRUE
) +
  labs(title = "Task Groupings (PCA Variable Factor Map)") +
  theme_bw()

7.4 Component Loadings (PC1-PC3)

loadings_df <- as.data.frame(round(pca_result$rotation[, 1:3], 3))

loadings_df %>%
  kable(caption = "PCA Loadings - First Three Components") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  row_spec(which(abs(loadings_df$PC1) > 0.3), bold = TRUE)
PCA Loadings - First Three Components
PC1 PC2 PC3
SEPR_AO_m_oddball 0.308 0.249 -0.099
SEPR_AO_m_standard -0.021 -0.114 0.364
RSS_SEPR_early_mean_control 0.280 -0.411 0.427
RSS_SEPR_early_mean_transition -0.212 0.240 0.154
mean_diff_RAND_to_REG1 -0.514 0.207 0.152
mean_diff_REG_to_RAND 0.134 0.272 -0.537
mean_diff_RAND_to_REG10 -0.469 0.316 0.070
SEPR_mean_cued -0.374 -0.373 -0.249
SEPR_mean_standard -0.226 -0.389 -0.416
CEPR_CUED_mean_cued -0.298 -0.254 0.189
CEPR_CUED_mean_standard -0.008 -0.357 -0.255

8 Demographic & Clinical Associations

8.1 Merge Demographic Data

demo_vars <- demo_data %>%
  rename(id = ID) %>%
  select(id, sex, CBCL_T_INT, CBCL_T_EXT, CBCL_T_GES, IQ_verbal_z, IQ_nonverbal_z)

bps_matrix_d     <- bps_matrix     %>% inner_join(demo_vars, by = "id")
df_cor_rpd_raw_d <- df_cor_rpd_raw %>% inner_join(demo_vars, by = "id")

cat("N with complete demographic data:", nrow(bps_matrix_d), "\n")
## N with complete demographic data: 97

8.2 Correlations: BPS x Demographics

# Setup data
cor_data      <- bps_matrix_d %>%
  mutate(sex = as.numeric(as.factor(sex))) %>%
  select(-id)

bps_demo_cor  <- Hmisc::rcorr(as.matrix(cor_data), type = "spearman")
bps_p <- bps_demo_cor$P
diag(bps_p) <- 1 

# 1. Plot without automatic coefficients
corrplot(
  bps_demo_cor$r,
  method      = "color",
  type        = "upper",
  tl.col      = "black",
  tl.srt      = 45,
  tl.cex      = 0.8,
  addCoef.col = NULL,      # Disabled to prevent overlap
  p.mat       = bps_p,
  sig.level   = 0.05,
  insig       = "label_sig",
  col         = colorRampPalette(c("#2166AC", "white", "#B2182B"))(200),
  title       = "BPS x Demographics (Spearman, stars = p < .05)",
  mar         = c(0, 0, 2, 0)
)

# 2. Add coefficients manually with an offset
n_bps <- ncol(bps_demo_cor$r)
for (i in 1:(n_bps-1)) {
  for (j in (i+1):n_bps) {
    text(x = j, 
         y = n_bps - i + 1, 
         labels = sprintf("%.2f", bps_demo_cor$r[i, j]), 
         pos = 1,      # Places text BELOW the center/star
         offset = 0.7, # Adjust this if you want the number higher or lower
         cex = 0.65, 
         col = "black")
  }
}

8.3 Correlations: SEPR x Demographics

# Setup data
cor_df        <- df_cor_rpd_raw_d %>%
  mutate(sex = as.numeric(as.factor(sex))) %>%
  select(-id)

sepr_demo_cor <- Hmisc::rcorr(as.matrix(cor_df), type = "spearman")
sepr_p <- sepr_demo_cor$P
diag(sepr_p) <- 1 

# 1. Plot without automatic coefficients
corrplot(
  sepr_demo_cor$r,
  method      = "color",
  type        = "upper",
  tl.col      = "black",
  tl.srt      = 45,
  tl.cex      = 0.75,
  addCoef.col = NULL,      # Disabled to prevent overlap
  p.mat       = sepr_p,
  sig.level   = 0.05,
  insig       = "label_sig",
  col         = colorRampPalette(c("#2166AC", "white", "#B2182B"))(200),
  title       = "SEPR x Demographics (Spearman, stars = p < .05)",
  mar         = c(0, 0, 2, 0)
)

# 2. Add coefficients manually with an offset
n_sepr <- ncol(sepr_demo_cor$r)
for (i in 1:(n_sepr-1)) {
  for (j in (i+1):n_sepr) {
    text(x = j, 
         y = n_sepr - i + 1, 
         labels = sprintf("%.2f", sepr_demo_cor$r[i, j]), 
         pos = 1,      # Places text BELOW the center/star
         offset = 0.7, # Adjust as needed
         cex = 0.6, 
         col = "black")
  }
}
